source.title: The algorithmic architecture of exploration in the human brain

confidence_rationale: Authoritative review synthesising the random-vs-directed-exploration literature. Explicitly states the dual-system conclusion: humans use a COMBINATION of random and uncertainty-directed exploration strategies that rely on different brain systems, have different developmental trajectories, and are sensitive to different task manipulations. The synthesis claim is the load-bearing one for Q-043 — the question of how to weight the two mechanisms presupposes that both are real, and Schulz & Gershman 2019 collect the cumulative evidence for that premise across behaviour, neuroimaging, pharmacology, and TMS.
failure_signatures: As a review, Schulz & Gershman 2019 does not add primary evidence; it synthesises Daw 2006 + Wilson 2014 + Zajkowski 2017 + Warren 2017 + Gershman 2018, all of which are sibling entries in this lit-pull. The independent evidence weight should be the primary papers, not this review — to avoid double-counting I keep the confidence moderate.
The review does not commit to a calibration verdict — it endorses the dual-system architecture but leaves the relative-weight question open. For Q-043 this is informative-but-non-resolving.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Humans use a combination of random and uncertainty-directed exploration strategies. The two strategies rely on different neural systems (LC-NE for random, frontopolar for directed), have different developmental trajectories (adolescent peak in random; adult-stable in directed), and are dissociably impaired by different perturbations.
mapping.ree_translation: The dual-system architecture Schulz & Gershman 2019 endorses is the same dual-system architecture REE implements as MECH-313/MECH-314. This review provides the synthesis-level argument that R1 of any future Q-043 falsifier should not be 'do both mechanisms exist?' — that question is already settled in the human literature. R1 should be 'what is the optimal calibration for THIS substrate (SD-054) and THIS task (rule discrimination)?'
mapping.mapping_caveat: Reviews aggregate primary evidence and inherit its caveats. The developmental angle (adolescent peak in random exploration) is interesting context but does not transfer cleanly to a substrate that does not have life-stages. The neural-system mappings (LC-NE for random, frontopolar for directed) are the most useful translation for REE.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review, Curr Opin Neurobiol, synthesises primary literature on human exploration algorithms 2006-2018.

confidence_components.notes: Curr Opin Neurobiol is a respected review venue; Schulz & Gershman are leading authors in this area. Mapping fidelity high at the synthesis level. The 0.76 aggregate reflects deliberate down-weighting to avoid double-counting the primary evidence that the review aggregates — the independent contribution of a review entry is the framing and the cross-paper synthesis, not new data.
